skill.name: torchdrug
skill.description: Guidance for using TorchDrug to build PyTorch graph neural network workflows for molecules, proteins, knowledge graphs, molecular generation, and retrosynthesis.
skill.summary: Build TorchDrug workflows for molecular and protein graph learning.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported code execution, shell execution, C2, sensitive-data, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples, PyTorch evaluation calls, dataset names, and scientific terminology. No evidence found for malicious code execution, credential access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration; only external documentation and project links remain as low-risk network indicators.

low_findings.title: Static Code Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported eval finding is task.eval(), a normal PyTorch evaluation-mode call, not dynamic code execution. The reported shell execution findings are Markdown code fences and inline code references in documentation, not executable Ruby or shell backticks. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: Line-numbered review shows documentation examples and PyTorch API usage with no user-controlled eval(), subprocess call, or command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows documentation examples and PyTorch API usage with no user-controlled eval(), subprocess call, or command invocation.

low_findings.title: Security Keyword Findings Are Scientific Dataset Terms
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The critical sensitive-data, C2, and weak-crypto detections map to dataset names and scientific terms such as SAMPL, ZINC, HIV, QM datasets, and drug-discovery wording. They do not reference Windows credential stores, command-and-control behavior, or cryptographic implementation. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: Reviewed contexts are dataset tables and task descriptions, with no credential paths, cryptographic code, malware instructions, or operational network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed contexts are dataset tables and task descriptions, with no credential paths, cryptographic code, malware instructions, or operational network behavior.

low_findings.title: External Links And Hosted Platform Recommendation
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE_LOW. The skill includes external links to TorchDrug documentation, GitHub, and an optional K-Dense Web recommendation for complex workflows. This is not automatic network access or exfiltration, but marketplace reviewers should note the promotional external destination. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The links and recommendation are explicit text in the documentation, with no code that opens a connection or transfers user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The links and recommendation are explicit text in the documentation, with no code that opens a connection or transfers user data.

content.value_statement: Drug discovery modeling needs reliable guidance across molecules, proteins, and knowledge graphs. This skill gives TorchDrug workflows, model choices, datasets, and evaluation patterns.
actual_capabilities: Select TorchDrug datasets for molecular, protein, knowledge graph, and retrosynthesis tasks.
Compare GNN, protein, knowledge graph, and generative model architectures for scientific workflows.
Outline training loops for property prediction, link prediction, molecular generation, and retrosynthesis.
Explain TorchDrug data structures, task interfaces, registry patterns, and model configuration concepts.
Recommend evaluation metrics, data splits, and validation checks for common drug discovery experiments.
Guide integration choices with PyTorch, PyTorch Lightning, RDKit, AlphaFold, and ESM.
limitations: It provides documentation guidance, not a running TorchDrug environment.
It does not verify biological claims or replace experimental validation.
It may require local package installation and external dataset downloads.
It is optimized for TorchDrug, not every cheminformatics or bioinformatics library.

use_cases.title: Plan Molecular Property Prediction
use_cases.description: Choose datasets, models, losses, metrics, and splits for ADMET or toxicity prediction.

use_cases.title: Design Protein Modeling Workflows
use_cases.description: Map protein sequence or structure tasks to TorchDrug models and evaluation methods.

use_cases.title: Prototype Drug Discovery GNNs
use_cases.description: Compare model families and build a practical TorchDrug experiment plan.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Starter Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to use TorchDrug for a molecular property prediction task. Recommend a beginner workflow, dataset, model, split, and metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Model Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare TorchDrug model choices for my task: [describe molecules, proteins, or knowledge graph]. Explain tradeoffs and a recommended baseline.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Experiment Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this TorchDrug experiment plan for dataset leakage, metric choice, model fit, training risk, and missing validation steps: [paste plan].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Multi-Stage Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a TorchDrug pipeline that combines property prediction, molecular generation, and retrosynthesis. Include datasets, models, metrics, and validation gates.

output_examples.input: Recommend a TorchDrug setup for blood-brain barrier prediction.
output_examples.output: Use the BBBP dataset, scaffold splitting, a GIN baseline, binary cross-entropy, and AUROC plus AUPRC for evaluation.

output_examples.input: I need a protein function prediction workflow.
output_examples.output: Start with GeneOntology or EnzymeCommission, choose ESM for sequence inputs or GearNet for structures, then evaluate multi-label performance.

output_examples.input: How should I validate generated molecules?
output_examples.output: Track validity, uniqueness, novelty, property scores, and RDKit checks before using candidates in downstream prediction or synthesis planning.

best_practices: Use scaffold splits for molecular benchmarks when generalization matters.
Start with a simple baseline before using large protein or generative models.
Validate generated molecules and synthesis routes with chemistry-aware checks.
anti_patterns: Do not report random-split results as realistic drug discovery performance.
Do not choose a model before matching the task type and input structure.
Do not treat generated molecules as useful without validity and feasibility review.

faq.question: What is TorchDrug best suited for?
faq.answer: It is best for PyTorch-based graph learning in molecules, proteins, knowledge graphs, generation, and retrosynthesis.

faq.question: Does this skill run TorchDrug code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users need their own Python environment to run experiments.

faq.question: Can it help choose datasets?
faq.answer: Yes. It summarizes TorchDrug datasets for molecular properties, proteins, knowledge graphs, and synthesis tasks.

faq.question: Can it replace RDKit or DeepChem?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on TorchDrug workflows and may recommend related tools when they fit the task.

faq.question: Is this useful for protein structure tasks?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers sequence and structure models, including ESM and GearNet-oriented workflows.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare the task goal, input data type, target labels, available compute, and validation requirements.
